sessions.intent: Carve plan for new code.run feature: Compiler/Runtime split + action handler + test plan handoff. Re-rooted from runtime2-cleanup onto its own branch off a9791ad5.

before.assumptions: code.run is a new PLang action that compiles a .cs file at runtime and invokes a method in it. App.Code (provider registry) stays untouched; new App.Compiler peer owns compile+cache. Roslyn already a transitive dep via Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Scripting 4.13.0 in PLang.csproj. The runtime2-cleanup branch already has the [Provider]→[Code] attribute rename (a9791ad5), which is why this branch bases there rather than on origin/runtime2 directly — the writing-modules doc references [Code].
before.risk: Sandboxing / signed-script trust is deferred to a separate pass and explicitly noted. Without it, code.run runs with the App's full trust — fine for trusted contexts, not for untrusted goal sources.

actions.context: New living doc: how to write a PLang module. There was no module-authoring doc when the session opened. Aligned with [Code] attribute (formerly [Provider]) per coder v29 rename. Live-additions tail logs two entries from this session (Path-as-string miss, ScriptEntry OBP smell).

Plan tree in canonical architect layout: spine + stages at the architect root, deep-dives under plan/. Three stages: Runtime leaf → Compiler with cache → action handler. Test strategy + coverage matrix + new-surfaces inventory for test-designer. Turn-by-turn design transcript (preserves the rejected paths and the why).

Coder file plan (NOT written this session — they are the deliverables for stages 1–3). Compiler/Runtime split is the OBP-correct shape after the design pivot in turn 9 of the transcript. App.Code is intentionally untouched.

after.status: plan delivered to test-designer and coder; pending implementation
after.health: green — branch self-contained, rooted off a9791ad5 so [Code] attribute and the runtime2-cleanup foundation are in place
after.notes: Two architect misses caught and logged as living-doc entries (Path-as-string miss, ScriptEntry OBP smell). Sandboxing/trust deferred to a separate design pass.

sessions.intent: Implement code.run with the minimal shape Ingi sketched live. Architect's larger Compiler/Runtime/Cache plan was deliberately set aside in conversation — coder followed Ingi's pseudocode (Runner facade + Path.GetContent + Path-stays-in-envelope) instead.

before.branch_base: started on code-run off runtime2-cleanup @ 3c7c827d; moved here onto modules.code.run/v1 (off a9791ad5 + architect plan) at session end
assumptions: Ingi's pseudocode is the contract: var code = new Runner(Path); return await code.Start(data)
Path stays in its Data<Path> envelope — never decomposed via .Value at the caller
Roslyn (Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Scripting 4.13.0) already a dep of PLang.csproj
Path constructor should accept relative paths and self-resolve when Context is supplied — Resolve becomes a one-liner

actions.context: Five new files + one new partial under App.Code. PluginLoadContext is a collectible ALC. Compiled wraps bytes and owns Load() → Data<Runtime>. Runtime wraps assembly + entry type, exposes Start(Data, Context) + Invoke(name, args, Context). Runner is a path-aware facade — one method, Start(Data) — so the action handler is two real lines. this.Load.cs is a partial on App.Code.@this that takes Data<Path> and dispatches .cs (compile via Roslyn) vs .dll (raw bytes). Action handler at modules/code/run.cs.

Two changes: (1) Path constructor now accepts relative paths and self-resolves when Context is supplied, so scripts can write `new Path("notes.md", Context)`. Resolve() static remains as a thin delegating one-liner for the source generator. (2) New Path.GetContent() — Path owns reading itself, routing through the registered IFile provider. Scripts say `await path.GetContent()` instead of looking up IFile and constructing a file.read action.

10 TUnit tests: Start no-args, Start with Data, Invoke named method + positional args, Task non-generic unwrap, MethodNotFound, ArityMismatch, FileNotFound, CompileFailed, double-load independence, and Path.GetContent through IFile.

End-to-end PLang integration: `- run scripts/hello.cs, write to %msg%` resolves through builder → code.run handler → Runner → Compiled → Runtime → script's Start() → returns 'hello plang world'. Both .test.goal files pass.
Coder bot dir migrated from .bot/code-run/ when the architect rebased onto modules.code.run/v1. plan.md reflects the live-designed minimal shape (not the architect's spec).

Architect's design (Compiler+Runtime split as App peers, hash cache, eviction race, ValidateShape, ProbeConstructor, error factory files) was deliberately not implemented. Ingi pushed back live: 'why all this code?' → collapsed to the Runner facade, no cache (registry IS the cache when we want it), no pre-Roslyn shape walk, no constructor probe (try (Context), fall back to ()), no separate error factory. Method/Options dispatch was dropped in favor of a single Start(Data) contract — variation lives inside the script. If named-method dispatch is needed later, Runtime.Invoke is still public; only the action handler currently doesn't expose it.

after.status: code.run end-to-end working; both test suites green
after.health: C# 2762/2762 pass (baseline 2752 + 10 new); PLang 201/201 pass (baseline 199 + Hello + RunDefault)
notes: Branch was code-run during the build, moved to modules.code.run/v1 at the end so the architect's plan and the implementation share one branch tree.

Two design lessons logged: (1) don't decompose Data envelopes at caller — pass Data<Path> through, unwrap at the work site only; (2) async state machines run as Task<VoidTaskResult> internally so use the declared MethodInfo.ReturnType, not task.GetType(), to decide Task vs Task<T> unwrap.
Out of scope (still): named-method dispatch from the action handler, sandboxing/signed-script trust, programmatic facades for other modules (file.Save, llm.Query).
